ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for sugar-coated chocolate gem biscuits; closest defensible match is FOODON_03301710 (cookie/biscuit). Fancy Gems are small button-shaped chocolate biscuits with a hard sugar shell, which fall within the biscuit/cookie category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Biscuits and cookies) is the closest defensible category for sugar-coated chocolate gem biscuits. No exact FoodEx2 entry exists for this specific confection.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this specific confectionery product.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Fancy Gems is approximately 30 g (about 20 small button-shaped pieces), providing roughly 127 kcal and 16 g of sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Fancy Gems — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Fancy Gems (cr_id F11080026). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glucose biscuits and sugar-coated confectionery — analogous estimate based on plain sweet biscuits/cookies GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Fancy Gems in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the closest analogous food: plain sweet biscuits/cookies with sugar coating, which are reported in the range GI 65–75 in the University of Sydney GI Database. The very high sugar content (54.5 g/100g), zero dietary fibre, and refined wheat flour base support a high GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (70 × 24.5) / 100 = 17.2, rounded to 17.1 for 30 g serving (carbohydrate per serving = 81.81 × 0.30 = 24.54 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sugar content (54.5 g/100g) with zero dietary fibre drives insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain, independently elevating blood pressure risk; sodium is also moderate at 145 mg/100g.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Fancy Gems entirely; the extreme sugar load (54.5 g/100g) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and cardiovascular strain, and even a 30 g portion delivers over 16 g of sugar with no fibre offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 54.5 g/100g far exceeds the 20 g/100g diabetes threshold; carbohydrate is 81.8 g/100g with zero dietary fibre, producing a very high glycaemic load (GL ~17 per 30 g serving) that causes rapid and severe postprandial blood glucose spikes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes must avoid Fancy Gems; the combination of 54.5 g/100g sugar, 81.8 g/100g carbohydrate, and zero fibre creates an extremely high glycaemic load that will cause acute and severe blood glucose elevation even in small portions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Ultra-processed confectionery with very high added sugar (54.5 g/100g), likely saturated and trans fats from vegetable shortening, and zero fibre or protective micronutrients; consistent with dietary patterns associated with increased CVD risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid Fancy Gems; the very high sugar content, likely saturated fat from shortening, and absence of any cardioprotective nutrients (fibre, potassium, antioxidants) make this an unfavourable food for cardiovascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined sugar at 54.5 g/100g strongly promotes pro-inflammatory mediators (TNF-alpha, IL-6); likely trans fat from vegetable shortening further amplifies inflammatory pathways.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Fancy Gems entirely; the extreme sugar content and likely trans fat from shortening are strongly pro-inflammatory and may exacerbate joint inflammation and disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fancy Gems are small, button-shaped confectionery items consisting of a baked biscuit or chocolate centre encased in a brightly coloured hard sugar shell. They are a popular snack in Singapore and across Southeast Asia, commonly sold in small bags and enjoyed as a sweet treat by children and adults alike. The texture combines a slightly crisp sugar coating with a soft, sweet interior. Nutritionally, Fancy Gems are an ultra-processed, high-sugar food: per 100 g they provide 423 kcal, 81.8 g carbohydrate (of which 54.5 g is sugar), 6.8 g fat, 4.5 g protein, and 145 mg sodium, with zero dietary fibre. The very high sugar content and negligible fibre result in a high glycaemic index (estimated ~70) and a high glycaemic load (~17 per 30 g serving), making them clinically unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The absence of fibre, potassium, and protective micronutrients means there is no nutritional offset to the high sugar and energy density. Fancy Gems are typically consumed as a snack or occasional treat and are not recommended as part of a health-conscious diet. They are classified as ultra-processed confectionery and are likely to contain vegetable shortening as the primary fat source, which may contribute saturated and trans fats.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in Fancy Gems?
Are Fancy Gems suitable for hypertension?
Are chocolate gems high in sugar?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11080026) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The food identity is unambiguous. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on closely matched sweet biscuit/cookie category, clearly documented. Fatty acid breakdown (saturated, monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat) is not reported in the HPB source and is set to -1 accordingly.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source (0.0 g/100g reported). calcium_mg and iron_mg are confirmed zero per HPB source (0.0 g/100g reported for both). saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for Fancy Gems are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (F11080026) as of 2026-04-16. The input dietary_tags included 'wholegrain', which is not supported by the nutritional profile (zero fibre, refined wheat flour base) and has been removed. The GI value of 70 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Fancy Gems was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Halal status is not confirmed from the HPB source and is set to false pending label verification.
